data.content: The bug is on line 29 — `ProcessPayment` only checks for zero, not negative amounts. The fastest workflow is `dotnet watch test`, which auto-reruns tests on every file save. Let me set it up:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

data.reasoningText:
The bug is clear: `ProcessPayment` only checks for `amount == 0` but doesn't check for negative amounts. The fix is to add `amount <= 0` or add a separate check for negative amounts.

The user asked about the fastest workflow for iterating. The answer is `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests automatically on file save. Let me set that up and also fix the bug.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-6d78e1daa7104b7191cbf3eec5db3c85 && dotnet watch test --filter \"ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException\" 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for the failing test", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch-test"}

toolArgs.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

data.content: Missing `using System;`. Let me fix that and the bug in one go:

arguments.old_str:
if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
        // Missing: validation for negative amounts
arguments.new_str: if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount must be positive");

data.content: Tests succeeded! Let me stop the watcher and show the final file:

All 3 tests pass. Here's the summary:

**The fix:** Changed `amount == 0` → `amount <= 0` on line 30 to reject negative amounts too. Also added `using System;` (needed for `ArgumentException`).

**The fast workflow:** Use `dotnet watch test` — it monitors your files and **auto-reruns tests on every save**, no manual rebuild needed. To filter to just your failing test:

```
dotnet watch test --filter "ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException"
```

This gives you a tight edit → save → see results loop without ever leaving the terminal.
